title: test(e2e): Expand webhook/CEL rejection matrix for Keystone
description:
# GitHub Issue #281: test(e2e): Expand webhook/CEL rejection matrix for Keystone CR (#277 Phase 1)

## Context

Parent: #277, Phase 1 → *Webhook / CEL validation matrix*.

Today `tests/e2e/keystone/invalid-cr/` covers exactly two rejection cases: invalid cron in `spec.fernet.rotationSchedule` and duplicate `plugins[*].configSection` values (see `tests/e2e/keystone/invalid-cr/chainsaw-test.yaml`, REQ-008/009 under CC-0012).

The validation rules in `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_types.go` (CEL via `+kubebuilder:validation:XValidation`) and `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_webhook.go` (defense-in-depth) cover considerably more misconfigurations than that. This sub-issue closes the E2E gap for every rejection path that is already implemented.

## Scope — new rejection cases

The existing test is extended with eleven input files and matching `apply` steps. Each row is taken 1:1 from the Phase 1 checklist in #277:

| # | Scenario | Enforced by | Expected `fieldPath` / key string |
|---|----------|-------------|------------------------------------|
| 1 | `spec.database` with **both** `clusterRef` and `host` set | CEL on `DatabaseSpec` (keystone_types.go:61) + webhook (keystone_webhook.go:195) | `spec.database` / `exactly one of clusterRef or host must be set` |
| 2 | `spec.cache` with **both** `clusterRef` and `servers` set | CEL on `CacheSpec` (types.go:66) + webhook (webhook.go:185) | `spec.cache` / `exactly one of clusterRef or servers must be set` |
| 3 | `spec.autoscaling` with neither `targetCPUUtilization` nor `targetMemoryUtilization` | CEL on `AutoscalingSpec` (types.go:207) + webhook | `spec.autoscaling` / `at least one of targetCPUUtilization or targetMemoryUtilization` |
| 4 | `spec.policyOverrides` with neither `rules` nor `configMapRef` | CEL (types.go:100) + webhook | `spec.policyOverrides` / `at least one of rules or configMapRef must be set` |
| 5 | `spec.policyOverrides.rules` with an empty key | CEL (types.go:101) + webhook | `spec.policyOverrides.rules` / `policy rule name must not be empty` |
| 6 | `spec.networkPolicy.ingress: []` (empty) | CEL on `NetworkPolicySpec` (types.go:236) + webhook | `spec.networkPolicy.ingress` / `at least one ingress source must be specified` |
| 7 | `spec.replicas: 0` **and** `spec.replicas: -1` (two variants) | `+kubebuilder:validation:Minimum=1` + webhook | `spec.replicas` / `replicas must be at least 1` |
| 8 | `spec.autoscaling.minReplicas > spec.autoscaling.maxReplicas` | webhook (webhook.go ~428) | `spec.autoscaling.minReplicas` / `must not exceed maxReplicas` |
| 9 | `spec.fernet.maxActiveKeys: 2` **and** `spec.credentialKeys.maxActiveKeys: 2` | `+kubebuilder:validation:Minimum=3` + webhook | `spec.fernet.maxActiveKeys` / `must be at least 3` (same for `credentialKeys`) |

> Note: the parent checklist says `maxActiveKeys < 2`, but the code enforces `>= 3`. The test follows the code; the parent checklist will be corrected when the box is ticked.

## Explicitly out of scope (prerequisites, separate tickets)

Three checkboxes from Phase 1 / "Webhook matrix" require webhook features that do **not** exist today. They need feature tickets first; the E2E tests follow once the feature lands (see #277, *Out of scope*):

- Empty / malformed `spec.image.tag` — `commonv1.ImageSpec.Tag` (`internal/common/types/types.go:14`) has neither `MinLength` nor a regex pattern.
- `topologySpreadConstraints[*].maxSkew: 0` — upstream `corev1.TopologySpreadConstraint.MaxSkew` carries no CRD-level minimum; no defense-in-depth in the Keystone webhook today.
- Mutation of immutable fields (`spec.database.clusterRef`, analogously `spec.cache.clusterRef`) — `ValidateUpdate` does not currently compare old vs. new.

Those three points will be split out of the Phase 1 list into their own `feat(CC-xxxx): harden <field> validation` issues, each followed by a dedicated test.

## Test design

Location: `tests/e2e/keystone/invalid-cr/` (extend the existing test — **no** new directory; every case shares the scope "webhook rejects an otherwise minimal Keystone CR").

File layout after the change:

```
tests/e2e/keystone/invalid-cr/
├── 00-invalid-cron.yaml                             (existing)
├── 01-duplicate-plugins.yaml                        (existing)
├── 02-database-both-modes.yaml                      (new)
├── 03-cache-both-modes.yaml                         (new)
├── 04-autoscaling-no-target.yaml                    (new)
├── 05-policy-overrides-no-source.yaml               (new)
├── 06-policy-overrides-empty-rule-key.yaml          (new)
├── 07-networkpolicy-empty-ingress.yaml              (new)
├── 08-replicas-zero.yaml                            (new)
├── 09-replicas-negative.yaml                        (new)
├── 10-hpa-min-greater-than-max.yaml                 (new)
├── 11-fernet-maxactivekeys-below-minimum.yaml       (new)
├── 12-credentialkeys-maxactivekeys-below-minimum.yaml (new)
└── chainsaw-test.yaml                               (extended)
```

Assertion pattern per step (identical to `00-invalid-cron.yaml`, so CI reports stay homogeneous):

```yaml
- try:
  - apply:
      file: 02-database-both-modes.yaml
      expect:
      - check:
          ($error != null): true
          (contains($error, 'spec.database')): true
          (contains($error, 'exactly one of clusterRef or host must be set')): true
```

Input YAMLs: each file carries a **complete, otherwise valid** minimal CR where exactly **one** field violates the rule under test. That keeps the error-message assertion sharp and makes follow-on regressions (e.g. a new required field) fail loudly. Template: the existing `00-invalid-cron.yaml` / `01-duplicate-plugins.yaml`.

All CRs remain **unapplied** in cluster state (admission reject), so no cleanup steps and no `delete` blocks are needed. The test fits inside the 30 s `apply` timeout from `tests/e2e/chainsaw-config.yaml` and does not affect `parallel: 4` execution.

## Definition of Done

- [ ] `chainsaw test tests/e2e/keystone/invalid-cr` green locally against `make kind-up`
- [ ] `make e2e` green (rest of the Keystone suite unchanged, no new flakes)
- [ ] Parallel-4 CI job `e2e` green in at least two consecutive runs (anti-flake)
- [ ] All eleven new steps use the `(contains($error, …))` pattern from the existing test; no dependency on an exact HTTP-code string
- [ ] No new infra components in `deploy/kind/`, no deviations from `tests/e2e/chainsaw-config.yaml`
- [ ] Parent #277: the nine covered checkboxes ticked, the three prerequisite points linked as follow-up issues
- [ ] Commit prefix `test(CC-xxxx)` in line with the repo style (feature code to be assigned by a maintainer)

## Risks / open questions

- **Error-message drift**: the CEL and webhook layers can produce slightly different strings (e.g. for `replicas: 0` the CRD layer says `"should be greater than or equal to 1"`, the webhook says `"replicas must be at least 1"`). Mitigation: match on the common substring (`"replicas"`) plus `($error != null): true`. Please confirm in review.
- **Validator ordering**: CRD schema → CEL → validating webhook. If CEL rejects first, the request never reaches the webhook and the webhook-specific string is absent from `$error`. The test therefore matches the semantic core message, not the producing layer.
- Feature code (`CC-xxxx`) to be assigned by a maintainer; suggest extending the sequence past CC-0093.

---
Source: https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/281

stories.title: Operator developer verifies all implemented webhook/CEL rules are exercised by E2E tests (CC-0094)
stories.want: every CEL XValidation and webhook.validate() rejection path in operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/ to have a corresponding Chainsaw E2E case under tests/e2e/keystone/invalid-cr/
stories.so_that: a silent regression (e.g. someone removing a CEL marker or a webhook guard during a refactor) fails a deterministic E2E step instead of shipping to production
criteria: The directory tests/e2e/keystone/invalid-cr/ contains exactly the files listed in the CC-0094 feature description: 00..12 plus chainsaw-test.yaml (11 new input manifests)
tests/e2e/keystone/invalid-cr/chainsaw-test.yaml has one `- try:` block per rejection case, in numeric filename order
Each new `- try:` block uses the identical shape as step 00-invalid-cron: `apply.file`, `expect.check.($error != null): true`, plus two (contains($error, ...)) assertions
All nine checklist items from #277 Phase 1 covered by CC-0094 map 1:1 to a chainsaw step (mapping documented in docs/reference/keystone-crd.md invalid-cr Suite table)

stories.title: SRE/maintainer detects rejection-path regressions before merge (CC-0094)
stories.want: `make e2e` and the GitHub Actions `e2e` job to fail loudly when any covered rejection rule stops rejecting
stories.so_that: a PR that accidentally weakens validation (deleted CEL rule, short-circuited webhook branch) cannot land on main
criteria: `chainsaw test tests/e2e/keystone/invalid-cr` is the single entry-point to the whole matrix (no additional --test-file flags required)
Every step is independent — no step depends on cluster state produced by a previous step; all CRs are rejected at admission and never reach etcd, so no cleanup block is required
The suite fits inside the existing 30s `apply` timeout and the 4-way parallel execution from tests/e2e/chainsaw-config.yaml
The parent issue #277 Phase 1 checklist can be ticked for all nine in-scope items once the suite is green on two consecutive CI runs

stories.title: Reviewer trusts error-message assertions against layer drift (CC-0094)
stories.want: assertion substrings that match the semantic core message independent of which validation layer (CRD schema, CEL, webhook) produced the rejection
stories.so_that: a rule move between layers (e.g. tightening a CEL rule that today is only enforced in the webhook) does not silently turn a test into a test of the producing layer
criteria: For replicas: 0 / replicas: -1 the assertion matches only the substring 'replicas' (both CRD-layer 'should be greater than or equal to 1' and webhook 'replicas must be at least 1' pass)
For maxActiveKeys < 3 the assertion matches 'maxActiveKeys' only (both CRD-layer minimum and webhook 'maxActiveKeys must be at least 3' pass)
For the mutual-exclusivity, no-target, empty-ingress and no-source cases the assertion additionally matches the webhook message substring that CEL also produces verbatim, keeping the assertion sharp
Every step also asserts ($error != null): true so that an accidental admission acceptance is not swallowed by a permissive contains() check

stories.title: Parallel-4 CI run stays green and fast (CC-0094)
stories.want: the expanded invalid-cr suite to run inside the same job slot and namespace as today without increasing wall time or flakiness
stories.so_that: `make e2e` remains under its existing budget and the job does not contend for shared infrastructure
criteria: No new files under deploy/kind/; no new infra prerequisites declared in chainsaw-test.yaml (only admission, no reconcile)
Each CR carries a unique metadata.name so that two admission attempts within the same namespace do not alias at failure-reporting time
No step declares a `delete`, `assert`, or `wait` block beyond the single `apply` with `expect` — admission-only workflow
The parent suite listed in docs/reference/keystone-e2e-tests.md still has the same suite count (invalid-cr continues to appear as one entry, not many)

stories.title: Documentation reader finds the expanded rejection coverage (CC-0094)
stories.want: the invalid-cr Suite table in docs/reference/keystone-crd.md to list all eleven new steps with their trigger and expected error substrings
stories.so_that: when a cluster admin hits a validation rejection in the wild, the reference doc explains which E2E step proves the rule is intentional
criteria: docs/reference/keystone-crd.md §'invalid-cr Suite' table has one row per chainsaw step (13 rows total after the change)
Each new row cites: Step name, Manifest filename, Requirement short-name, Expected Error substring(s)
Feature ID CC-0094 appears in the front-matter `feature:` list of docs/reference/keystone-crd.md (alongside the existing CC-0011/CC-0012/...)
File-layout ascii tree at the end of the CRD doc lists the new YAMLs (02-...yaml through 12-...yaml) under tests/e2e/keystone/invalid-cr/

stories.title: Parent issue maintainer closes the Phase 1 checklist cleanly (CC-0094)
stories.want: the three out-of-scope prerequisite items (image.tag MinLength, topologySpreadConstraints maxSkew Minimum, clusterRef immutability on update) to be explicitly deferred with follow-up ticket stubs rather than silently dropped
stories.so_that: the Phase 1 checklist in #277 is fully resolved — either ticked or linked to a follow-up — with no ambiguity about what CC-0094 delivers
criteria: deferred_items in this plan enumerates the three prerequisite gaps with rationale and suggested ticket naming
No chainsaw input file or assertion attempts to test the deferred items (no image.tag-empty.yaml, no maxSkew-zero.yaml, no clusterRef-mutation step)
CC-0094 commit messages and PR description explicitly list the deferred items so reviewers can cross-check scope
The parent-issue PR comment at close time cites the follow-up ticket numbers for the three deferred items

requirements.description: The invalid-cr E2E suite SHALL reject a Keystone CR that sets both spec.database.clusterRef and spec.database.host (CC-0094)
requirements.rationale: CEL rule on KeystoneSpec.Database (keystone_types.go:61) and webhook defense-in-depth (keystone_webhook.go:195-203) both enforce mutual exclusivity; an E2E step pins the contract end-to-end

scenarios.name: Both modes set — rejected
scenarios.when: chainsaw applies tests/e2e/keystone/invalid-cr/02-database-both-modes.yaml which populates both spec.database.clusterRef.name and spec.database.host
scenarios.then: the API server returns an admission error; chainsaw captures it into $error
and_then: $error contains the substring 'exactly one of clusterRef or host must be set'

scenarios.name: Validator ordering tolerated
scenarios.when: CEL rejects the CR before the webhook runs
scenarios.then: the CEL rejection still contains the 'exactly one of clusterRef or host must be set' message (same string is embedded in the CEL XValidation `message:` attribute on KeystoneSpec.Database)

scenarios.name: Otherwise-valid CR isolates the rule
scenarios.when: the manifest sets every other required spec field (image, cache, fernet, bootstrap) to valid values
scenarios.then: only the dual-mode database produces the error; no unrelated required-field error appears
and_then: the assertion substrings are unique enough that a new required field on KeystoneSpec would fail loudly rather than silently shifting the rejection cause

requirements.description: The invalid-cr E2E suite SHALL reject a Keystone CR that sets both spec.cache.clusterRef and spec.cache.servers (CC-0094)
requirements.rationale: CEL rule on KeystoneSpec.Cache (keystone_types.go:66) and webhook (keystone_webhook.go:185-193) both enforce mutual exclusivity

scenarios.name: Both modes set — rejected
scenarios.when: chainsaw applies 03-cache-both-modes.yaml populating spec.cache.clusterRef.name AND a non-empty spec.cache.servers list
scenarios.then: admission rejects and $error contains 'spec.cache' and 'exactly one of clusterRef or servers must be set'

scenarios.name: Empty servers does not trigger
scenarios.when: the manifest carries clusterRef and an explicitly empty servers: [] array
scenarios.then: the CEL rule `has(self.clusterRef) != (has(self.servers) && size(self.servers) > 0)` is NOT triggered
and_then: Mitigation: the manifest therefore sets servers to a non-empty list so the rule fires deterministically

scenarios.name: Backend field remains valid
scenarios.when: spec.cache.backend is set to 'dogpile.cache.pymemcache' (the default)
scenarios.then: no unrelated cache-related error appears in $error

requirements.description: The invalid-cr E2E suite SHALL reject a Keystone CR whose spec.autoscaling sets neither targetCPUUtilization nor targetMemoryUtilization (CC-0094)
requirements.rationale: CEL rule on AutoscalingSpec (keystone_types.go:207) and webhook (keystone_webhook.go:461-466) both require at least one target

scenarios.name: No target set — rejected
scenarios.when: chainsaw applies 04-autoscaling-no-target.yaml with spec.autoscaling.maxReplicas=10 and both target*Utilization fields omitted
scenarios.then: admission rejects and $error contains 'spec.autoscaling' and 'at least one of targetCPUUtilization or targetMemoryUtilization'

scenarios.name: MaxReplicas valid — not a confounder
scenarios.when: spec.autoscaling.maxReplicas is at least 1 and >= spec.replicas
scenarios.then: the rejection is attributable solely to the missing utilization targets

scenarios.name: Validator ordering tolerated
scenarios.when: CEL rejects before the webhook runs
scenarios.then: CEL carries the identical message string (sourced from the XValidation annotation on AutoscalingSpec)

requirements.description: The invalid-cr E2E suite SHALL reject a Keystone CR whose spec.policyOverrides sets neither rules nor configMapRef (CC-0094)
requirements.rationale: CEL rule on KeystoneSpec.PolicyOverrides (keystone_types.go:100) and webhook (keystone_webhook.go:267-273) both require at least one source

scenarios.name: Empty policyOverrides — rejected
scenarios.when: chainsaw applies 05-policy-overrides-no-source.yaml with spec.policyOverrides: {} (object present, both fields absent)
scenarios.then: admission rejects and $error contains 'spec.policyOverrides' and 'at least one of rules or configMapRef must be set'

scenarios.name: Empty rules map triggers the rule
scenarios.when: spec.policyOverrides.rules: {} is set (empty map) and configMapRef is omitted
scenarios.then: the CEL guard `(has(self.rules) && size(self.rules) > 0) || self.configMapRef != null` rejects because size is zero
and_then: Chosen manifest form: omit both fields so that neither CEL nor webhook can accept

scenarios.name: Otherwise-valid CR
scenarios.when: every other required spec field is set correctly
scenarios.then: no unrelated error appears; the assertion substrings are uniquely attributable to the policyOverrides rule

requirements.description: The invalid-cr E2E suite SHALL reject a Keystone CR whose spec.policyOverrides.rules contains an empty map key (CC-0094)
requirements.rationale: CEL rule on KeystoneSpec.PolicyOverrides (keystone_types.go:101) and webhook (keystone_webhook.go:276-284) both forbid empty rule names

scenarios.name: Empty key in rules — rejected
scenarios.when: chainsaw applies 06-policy-overrides-empty-rule-key.yaml with spec.policyOverrides.rules: {'': 'role:admin'}
scenarios.then: admission rejects and $error contains 'spec.policyOverrides.rules' and 'policy rule name must not be empty'

scenarios.name: Non-empty key passes
scenarios.when: a non-empty key is added alongside the empty key
scenarios.then: the rule still fires (CEL `self.rules.all(k, k != '')` is violated by the presence of the empty key regardless of siblings)
and_then: Chosen manifest form: a single empty-key entry to keep the manifest minimal

scenarios.name: Chainsaw-safe YAML representation
scenarios.when: the empty key is serialized in YAML
scenarios.then: the manifest uses `'':` (quoted empty string) so the parser preserves the key

requirements.description: The invalid-cr E2E suite SHALL reject a Keystone CR whose spec.networkPolicy.ingress is an empty array (CC-0094)
requirements.rationale: CEL rule on NetworkPolicySpec (keystone_types.go:236) and webhook (keystone_webhook.go:471-476) both require at least one ingress source

scenarios.name: Empty ingress — rejected
scenarios.when: chainsaw applies 07-networkpolicy-empty-ingress.yaml with spec.networkPolicy.ingress: []
scenarios.then: admission rejects and $error contains 'spec.networkPolicy.ingress' and 'at least one ingress source'

scenarios.name: Pointer form of networkPolicy
scenarios.when: spec.networkPolicy is an object (not nil)
scenarios.then: the CEL rule on the inner NetworkPolicySpec triggers because the object is present

scenarios.name: additionalEgress irrelevance
scenarios.when: spec.networkPolicy.additionalEgress is omitted
scenarios.then: the rejection is attributable solely to the empty ingress, not to additionalEgress shape

requirements.description: The invalid-cr E2E suite SHALL reject a Keystone CR whose spec.replicas is 0 (CC-0094)
requirements.rationale: +kubebuilder:validation:Minimum=1 on KeystoneSpec.Replicas (keystone_types.go:52) plus defense-in-depth webhook check (keystone_webhook.go:157-163)

scenarios.name: Zero replicas — rejected
scenarios.when: chainsaw applies 08-replicas-zero.yaml with spec.replicas: 0 and all other fields valid
scenarios.then: admission rejects and $error contains 'replicas'
and_then: CRD-layer rejection message is 'should be greater than or equal to 1'; webhook message is 'replicas must be at least 1' — both contain the substring 'replicas'

scenarios.name: Layer-order tolerated
scenarios.when: the CRD schema rejects first because Minimum=1 is evaluated before the webhook
scenarios.then: the assertion intentionally matches only 'replicas' (no stronger substring) so either layer satisfies the test

scenarios.name: Defaulting does not mask the test
scenarios.when: the mutating webhook defaults Replicas=0 to 3 (keystone_webhook.go:80-82)
scenarios.then: the CRD schema `Minimum=1` runs AFTER defaulting — so an explicit spec.replicas:0 in the manifest may be defaulted away; the E2E step must therefore choose a value that defaulting preserves
and_then: Resolution: spec.replicas: 0 is defaulted → no rejection; to reliably hit the Minimum=1 rule the manifest uses spec.replicas: 0 AND relies on CRD-schema validation running BEFORE the mutating webhook for structural fields. Verify locally — if it is masked, flip this case to spec.replicas: -1 only and mark REQ-007 as subsumed by REQ-008.

requirements.description: The invalid-cr E2E suite SHALL reject a Keystone CR whose spec.replicas is negative (CC-0094)
requirements.rationale: +kubebuilder:validation:Minimum=1 on KeystoneSpec.Replicas is a structural CRD check that runs before any defaulting path can coerce a negative value

scenarios.name: Negative replicas — rejected
scenarios.when: chainsaw applies 09-replicas-negative.yaml with spec.replicas: -1
scenarios.then: admission rejects and $error contains 'replicas'
and_then: Defaulting at keystone_webhook.go:80 only fires for `Replicas == 0`; -1 passes through to Minimum=1 rejection

scenarios.name: Webhook fallback produces compatible substring
scenarios.when: for any reason CRD schema is bypassed and the webhook runs
scenarios.then: keystone_webhook.go:157-163 emits 'replicas must be at least 1' which also contains 'replicas'

scenarios.name: Isolation from defaulting
scenarios.when: the manifest sets spec.replicas: -1 and every other spec field is valid
scenarios.then: no other rejection masks the replicas error

requirements.description: The invalid-cr E2E suite SHALL reject a Keystone CR whose spec.autoscaling.minReplicas exceeds spec.autoscaling.maxReplicas (CC-0094)
requirements.rationale: Cross-field webhook check at keystone_webhook.go:428-434 with no equivalent CEL rule — this is a webhook-only case so the assertion must tolerate the webhook wording only

scenarios.name: Min > Max — rejected
scenarios.when: chainsaw applies 10-hpa-min-greater-than-max.yaml with spec.autoscaling.minReplicas=5 and spec.autoscaling.maxReplicas=3 (plus one targetCPUUtilization to pass REQ-003's rule first)
scenarios.then: admission rejects and $error contains 'spec.autoscaling.minReplicas' and 'must not exceed maxReplicas'

scenarios.name: Ensure prior rules do not short-circuit
scenarios.when: spec.autoscaling.targetCPUUtilization is set to a valid value (e.g. 70) in the manifest
scenarios.then: the no-target rule (REQ-003) does NOT fire; the min>max rule is the sole rejection cause

scenarios.name: Webhook-only rule
scenarios.when: no CEL rule exists for this pair
scenarios.then: the assertion substring 'must not exceed maxReplicas' matches only the webhook message
and_then: If the test ever fails because the API server short-circuits on a different rule, this is a regression — not an assertion relaxation candidate

requirements.description: The invalid-cr E2E suite SHALL reject a Keystone CR whose spec.fernet.maxActiveKeys is below the minimum of 3 (CC-0094)

scenarios.name: MaxActiveKeys=2 — rejected
scenarios.when: chainsaw applies 11-fernet-maxactivekeys-below-minimum.yaml with spec.fernet.maxActiveKeys: 2 (note: parent checklist says `<2` but the code enforces `>=3`; following the code)
scenarios.then: admission rejects and $error contains 'maxActiveKeys'
and_then: CRD message 'should be greater than or equal to 3' AND webhook message 'maxActiveKeys must be at least 3' both contain 'maxActiveKeys'

scenarios.name: Defaulting does not mask
scenarios.when: the mutating webhook defaults MaxActiveKeys=0 to 3 (keystone_webhook.go:83-85)
scenarios.then: the manifest uses 2 (not 0) so defaulting is not triggered and the Minimum=3 check fires

scenarios.name: Fernet.rotationSchedule valid
scenarios.when: spec.fernet.rotationSchedule is omitted (defaulted to '0 0 * * 0' by the CRD) or set to a valid cron
scenarios.then: no rotationSchedule-related rejection confounds this test

requirements.description: The invalid-cr E2E suite SHALL reject a Keystone CR whose spec.credentialKeys.maxActiveKeys is below the minimum of 3 (CC-0094)

scenarios.name: CredentialKeys.MaxActiveKeys=2 — rejected
scenarios.when: chainsaw applies 12-credentialkeys-maxactivekeys-below-minimum.yaml with spec.credentialKeys.maxActiveKeys: 2
scenarios.then: admission rejects and $error contains 'maxActiveKeys'

scenarios.name: Sibling fernet.maxActiveKeys stays valid
scenarios.when: spec.fernet.maxActiveKeys is omitted (CRD defaults to 3) or set to 3
scenarios.then: only credentialKeys triggers the rule — the error is attributable to credentialKeys
and_then: Because the assertion only matches 'maxActiveKeys', a second test case must exist (REQ-010) so that the split between fernet and credentialKeys is verifiable. Both tests share the substring but live in distinct files, so a regression that breaks one-but-not-the-other is still visible from which file failed.

scenarios.name: Defaulting does not mask
scenarios.when: maxActiveKeys is set to 2 (not 0)
scenarios.then: the mutating webhook does not coerce the value — CRD Minimum=3 rejects

requirements.description: Each new invalid-cr manifest SHALL carry a complete, otherwise-valid minimal Keystone CR differing from the valid baseline by exactly one rule-violating field (CC-0094)
requirements.rationale: Isolates the rule under test; makes it loud when an unrelated required-field change is added later (the new required field would cause every invalid-cr step to fail simultaneously until the baseline is updated)

scenarios.name: Baseline fields present
scenarios.when: the manifest declares spec.image.repository, spec.image.tag, spec.database, spec.cache, spec.fernet, spec.bootstrap
scenarios.then: only the field under test is invalid; every other required path is syntactically and semantically valid
and_then: The baseline mirrors the shape used by 00-invalid-cron.yaml so reviewers see a single canonical template

scenarios.name: metadata.name unique per file
scenarios.when: multiple invalid-cr manifests are applied concurrently (chainsaw parallel:4)
scenarios.then: each CR has a distinct metadata.name so reject messages are traceable to the originating file
and_then: Chosen names: db-both-modes, cache-both-modes, autoscaling-no-target, policy-no-source, policy-empty-key, networkpolicy-empty-ingress, replicas-zero, replicas-negative, hpa-min-gt-max, fernet-keys-below-min, credkeys-below-min

scenarios.name: No dependency on infrastructure CRDs
scenarios.when: the manifests only reference the Keystone CRD schema (no MariaDB/Memcached cluster CRs, no ConfigMaps, no Secrets)
scenarios.then: every rejection happens at admission of the Keystone CR itself; no secondary reference-lookup failure could confound the test
and_then: Exception: spec.bootstrap.adminPasswordSecretRef.name references a Secret that is never read because the CR is rejected — the Secret does not need to exist

requirements.description: The chainsaw-test.yaml SHALL use the identical assertion shape (($error != null): true plus two (contains($error, ...)) predicates) for every new case (CC-0094)
requirements.rationale: Homogeneous CI reporting; makes the suite mechanically readable and diffable when new cases are appended

scenarios.name: Identical try/apply/expect/check block per case
scenarios.when: a reviewer diffs chainsaw-test.yaml against the pre-CC-0094 version
scenarios.then: the diff is entirely new `- try:` blocks — no modifications to the two pre-existing blocks (00-invalid-cron, 01-duplicate-plugins)

scenarios.name: No dependency on exact HTTP status or code field
scenarios.when: the API server returns a 422 Invalid
scenarios.then: the assertion matches substrings in $error, not $error.code or $error.reason
and_then: Consistent with step 00/01 which also match on substrings only

scenarios.name: Step comments cite REQ-IDs and feature ID
scenarios.when: a maintainer reads chainsaw-test.yaml
scenarios.then: every new `- try:` block is preceded by a `# REQ-NNN (CC-0094):` comment naming the rule under test

requirements.description: docs/reference/keystone-crd.md §'invalid-cr Suite' SHALL list all eleven new steps with Step, Manifest, Requirement, Expected Error columns (CC-0094)
requirements.rationale: Existing invalid-cr Suite table has only two rows; a 9-checkbox Phase 1 expansion without a doc update would leave the table stale

scenarios.name: Table extended in place
scenarios.when: the reader opens docs/reference/keystone-crd.md and scrolls to 'invalid-cr Suite'
scenarios.then: the table has 13 data rows (2 existing + 11 new) in numeric filename order
and_then: Each new row follows the existing column format exactly

scenarios.name: Front-matter feature list updated
scenarios.when: controller-gen or tooling parses the front-matter
scenarios.then: the `feature:` key includes CC-0094 alongside CC-0011/CC-0012/...

scenarios.name: File-layout tree updated
scenarios.when: the reader scrolls to the file-layout block at the end of keystone-crd.md
scenarios.then: the tests/e2e/keystone/invalid-cr/ subtree lists all eleven new .yaml files

tasks.title: Add tests/e2e/keystone/invalid-cr/02-database-both-modes.yaml (otherwise-valid Keystone CR setting BOTH spec.database.clusterRef.name AND spec.database.host) and append its chainsaw step to chainsaw-test.yaml — assertions: ($error != null), contains 'spec.database', contains 'exactly one of clusterRef or host must be set' (REQ-001, REQ-012, REQ-013) — CC-0094

Add tests/e2e/keystone/invalid-cr/03-cache-both-modes.yaml (otherwise-valid Keystone CR setting BOTH spec.cache.clusterRef.name AND a non-empty spec.cache.servers list) and append its chainsaw step — assertions: ($error != null), contains 'spec.cache', contains 'exactly one of clusterRef or servers must be set' (REQ-002, REQ-012, REQ-013) — CC-0094

Add tests/e2e/keystone/invalid-cr/04-autoscaling-no-target.yaml (spec.autoscaling.maxReplicas=10, no targetCPUUtilization, no targetMemoryUtilization; spec.replicas kept <= maxReplicas) and append its chainsaw step — assertions: ($error != null), contains 'spec.autoscaling', contains 'at least one of targetCPUUtilization or targetMemoryUtilization' (REQ-003, REQ-012, REQ-013) — CC-0094

Add tests/e2e/keystone/invalid-cr/05-policy-overrides-no-source.yaml (spec.policyOverrides: {} — present but empty) and append its chainsaw step — assertions: ($error != null), contains 'spec.policyOverrides', contains 'at least one of rules or configMapRef must be set' (REQ-004, REQ-012, REQ-013) — CC-0094

Add tests/e2e/keystone/invalid-cr/06-policy-overrides-empty-rule-key.yaml (spec.policyOverrides.rules with a single entry whose key is the empty string '') and append its chainsaw step — assertions: ($error != null), contains 'spec.policyOverrides.rules', contains 'policy rule name must not be empty' (REQ-005, REQ-012, REQ-013) — CC-0094

Add tests/e2e/keystone/invalid-cr/07-networkpolicy-empty-ingress.yaml (spec.networkPolicy.ingress: []) and append its chainsaw step — assertions: ($error != null), contains 'spec.networkPolicy.ingress', contains 'at least one ingress source' (REQ-006, REQ-012, REQ-013) — CC-0094

Add tests/e2e/keystone/invalid-cr/08-replicas-zero.yaml (spec.replicas: 0) and append its chainsaw step — assertions: ($error != null), contains 'replicas' (loose match for CRD 'greater than or equal to 1' OR webhook 'replicas must be at least 1'); add a comment in the step noting the loose match rationale and that the reviewer must confirm defaulting does not mask it (REQ-007, REQ-012, REQ-013) — CC-0094

Add tests/e2e/keystone/invalid-cr/09-replicas-negative.yaml (spec.replicas: -1) and append its chainsaw step — assertions: ($error != null), contains 'replicas' (CRD Minimum=1 rejects deterministically because defaulter only triggers on ==0) (REQ-008, REQ-012, REQ-013) — CC-0094

Add tests/e2e/keystone/invalid-cr/10-hpa-min-greater-than-max.yaml (spec.autoscaling.minReplicas=5, maxReplicas=3, AND targetCPUUtilization=70 to avoid confounding with REQ-003; spec.replicas kept at 3 or below so the implicit-min>max rule at keystone_webhook.go:438 does not fire) and append its chainsaw step — assertions: ($error != null), contains 'spec.autoscaling.minReplicas', contains 'must not exceed maxReplicas' (REQ-009, REQ-012, REQ-013) — CC-0094

Add tests/e2e/keystone/invalid-cr/11-fernet-maxactivekeys-below-minimum.yaml (spec.fernet.maxActiveKeys: 2; rotationSchedule omitted so CRD default '0 0 * * 0' applies) and append its chainsaw step — assertions: ($error != null), contains 'maxActiveKeys' (loose match because CRD Minimum=3 wording differs from webhook wording) (REQ-010, REQ-012, REQ-013) — CC-0094

Add tests/e2e/keystone/invalid-cr/12-credentialkeys-maxactivekeys-below-minimum.yaml (spec.credentialKeys.maxActiveKeys: 2; fernet.maxActiveKeys defaulted to 3) and append its chainsaw step — assertions: ($error != null), contains 'maxActiveKeys' (REQ-011, REQ-012, REQ-013) — CC-0094

Audit the final tests/e2e/keystone/invalid-cr/chainsaw-test.yaml: verify (a) every new `- try:` block is preceded by a `# REQ-NNN (CC-0094):` comment identifying the rule under test, (b) all steps appear in numeric filename order 02..12 after the pre-existing 00/01 steps, (c) no `delete`/`assert`/`wait` blocks were introduced, (d) the top-level SPDX header remains unchanged (REQ-013) — CC-0094

Cross-review all eleven new manifests against the baseline template from 00-invalid-cron.yaml: (a) identical SPDX header, (b) `apiVersion: keystone.openstack.c5c3.io/v1alpha1`, `kind: Keystone`, (c) every required spec path (image, database, cache, fernet, bootstrap) present with the canonical minimal-valid values, (d) exactly one field violates the rule under test, (e) metadata.name distinct across all eleven files (REQ-012) — CC-0094

Extend docs/reference/keystone-crd.md §'invalid-cr Suite' table with 11 new rows (Step name, Manifest filename, Requirement one-line, Expected Error substrings) in numeric filename order; add CC-0094 to the front-matter `feature:` list; extend the file-layout ASCII tree at the end of the doc to include 02-...yaml through 12-...yaml (REQ-014) — CC-0094

Static verification (agent-local, sandbox): run `yamllint` or equivalent schema parse over the 11 new manifests AND `chainsaw-test.yaml`; verify each new manifest is schema-valid per the generated CRD at operators/keystone/config/crd/bases/keystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml EXCEPT for the one deliberately-violating field. Report any diagnostic findings without attempting kind-up (sandbox constraint — documented in memory) (REQ-012, REQ-013) — CC-0094

Live-cluster verification (HUMAN/CI, cannot run in sandbox): `make kind-up && chainsaw test tests/e2e/keystone/invalid-cr` — confirm all 13 steps (2 existing + 11 new) pass on the first run and on a second consecutive run (anti-flake). If any step flakes or if spec.replicas:0 is silently defaulted, follow the fallback in REQ-007 scenario 3 and the test spec for 08-replicas-zero.yaml — CC-0094

Parent-issue maintenance: on PR merge, tick the nine in-scope checkboxes under #277 Phase 1 and link the three deferred prerequisite items (spec.image.tag MinLength, topologySpreadConstraints maxSkew Minimum, clusterRef immutability on ValidateUpdate) to follow-up feat-tickets as described in deferred_items — CC-0094

[blocker] Every new step's (contains($error, ...)) predicate uses the specific substrings enumerated in the requirements (spec.database, spec.cache, spec.autoscaling, spec.policyOverrides, spec.policyOverrides.rules, spec.networkPolicy.ingress, spec.autoscaling.minReplicas, 'must not exceed maxReplicas', 'exactly one of ... must be set', 'at least one of ...', 'policy rule name must not be empty', 'at least one ingress source', 'replicas', 'maxActiveKeys') — no step relies on an exact HTTP code string (CC-0094 REQ-013)

[blocker] No production code (operators/**, internal/**, deploy/**) changed by the PR — this is a tests-and-docs only feature; if a production file appears in the diff the PR must be split (CC-0094 scope)

[critical] docs/reference/keystone-crd.md §'invalid-cr Suite' table has 13 rows in numeric filename order; front-matter `feature:` list includes CC-0094; file-layout ASCII tree lists the new YAML files (CC-0094 REQ-014)
[minor] Each new chainsaw `- try:` block carries a `# REQ-NNN (CC-0094):` lead comment so the step-to-requirement mapping is readable from the file alone (CC-0094 REQ-013)

implementation_notes:
ARCHITECTURE / SCOPE MODE (Standard): pure E2E test expansion. No changes to operators/** or internal/**. The only production-adjacent change is the addition of CC-0094 to the `feature:` front-matter of docs/reference/keystone-crd.md and new rows in the invalid-cr Suite table.

DATA FLOW (admission path exercised per step):
  kubectl apply (chainsaw)
    → kube-apiserver: OpenAPI/structural schema check (includes +kubebuilder:validation:Minimum markers)
      → CRD CEL XValidation (keystone_types.go: DatabaseSpec, CacheSpec, AutoscalingSpec, PolicySpec, NetworkPolicySpec)
        → Mutating webhook: Default() (keystone_webhook.go:79-131 — only rewrites zero/nil fields)
          → Validating webhook: validate() (keystone_webhook.go:151-573 — defense-in-depth)
            → Rejection flows back to chainsaw as $error (string body of the API machinery response)

LAYER-ORDERING: the CRD schema layer runs BEFORE CEL, which runs BEFORE the mutating webhook, which runs BEFORE the validating webhook. Consequences:
  - spec.replicas: 0 → defaulting at keystone_webhook.go:80 rewrites to 3 IF the mutating webhook runs before Minimum=1 structural validation. In Kubernetes 1.25+ the structural schema check runs BEFORE mutating webhooks for known typed fields, so Minimum=1 should reject first. If this ordering has changed in the target cluster version, the fallback recorded in REQ-007 scenario 3 kicks in.
  - spec.replicas: -1 → never defaulted (defaulter only triggers on == 0); Minimum=1 rejects deterministically.
  - maxActiveKeys: 2 → never defaulted (defaulter only triggers on == 0); Minimum=3 rejects deterministically.
  - database/cache mutual exclusivity: CEL rejects first with the identical message string (the XValidation `message:` field carries the same text the webhook uses), so the loose substring match works for both layers.

OTHERWISE-VALID BASELINE (target template, copied from 00-invalid-cron.yaml with the offending field swapped):
  apiVersion: keystone.openstack.c5c3.io/v1alpha1
  kind: Keystone
  metadata:
    name: <case-specific>
  spec:
    replicas: 3
    image:
      repository: ghcr.io/c5c3/keystone
      tag: "2025.2"
    database:
      host: db.example.com
      port: 3306
      database: keystone
      secretRef:
        name: keystone-db
    cache:
      backend: dogpile.cache.pymemcache
      servers: ["mc:11211"]
    fernet:
      rotationSchedule: "0 0 * * 0"
      maxActiveKeys: 3
    bootstrap:
      adminUser: admin
      adminPasswordSecretRef:
        name: keystone-admin
      region: RegionOne

The Secret referenced in spec.bootstrap.adminPasswordSecretRef does NOT need to exist in the test namespace — the CR is rejected at admission and never reaches the reconciler that would look the Secret up.

ASSERTION ROBUSTNESS STRATEGY:
  - Two (contains($error, ...)) predicates per step is the target, matching step 00-invalid-cron's shape.
  - For webhook-only rules (REQ-009 min>max) both predicates match the webhook wording — a regression that moves the rule to CEL with a different message is loud by design.
  - For dual-layer rules (REQ-001..REQ-006) the predicates use the shared message string that both layers emit.
  - For Minimum-marker rules (REQ-007, REQ-008, REQ-010, REQ-011) ONE predicate matches only the field-name substring; the second predicate matches ($error != null) — because the CRD Minimum wording 'should be greater than or equal to N' is not under operator control and could change between Kubernetes versions.

PITFALL REGISTRY:
  (a) Empty key in a YAML map — use single-quoted empty string ''. Some YAML parsers are lenient; verifying via the CRD generation path is the only reliable check.
  (b) spec.cache.servers empty list — the CEL rule only fires when size > 0, so the dual-mode manifest must use a non-empty servers list.
  (c) Defaulter mutation — maxActiveKeys=0 and replicas=0 are rewritten by Default(); tests must use 2 and -1 respectively to avoid being silently repaired.
  (d) HPA implicit-min rule — if minReplicas is absent, the reconciler defaults it to spec.replicas, and keystone_webhook.go:438 rejects when that implicit default exceeds maxReplicas. The REQ-009 manifest therefore sets minReplicas explicitly AND keeps spec.replicas below maxReplicas.
  (e) PolicyOverrides empty object — `spec.policyOverrides: {}` with both rules and configMapRef absent triggers both CEL and webhook. An absent pointer would skip both.
  (f) Sandbox limitation — per memory, the local environment has no docker/cluster. Task 4.1 performs the static-render verification; Task 4.2 is marked as a human/CI step.

PATTERN: the chainsaw-test.yaml shape is already the repo canon (see tests/e2e/keystone/invalid-cr/chainsaw-test.yaml pre-CC-0094). No new pattern file needs to be created — this PR is pure pattern replication.
